growth_records.cite: Taubner et al. Lipidomics and Comparative Metabolite Excretion Analysis of Methanogenic Archaea Reveal Organism-Specific Adaptations to Varying Temperatures and Substrate Concentrations. mSystems (2023). DOI:10.1128/msystems.01159-22. [PMC10134847]
growth_records.snippet: MER_mean 3.875 +/- 0.237 ... WER_mean 8.056 +/- 0.419 ... Mean total AAER 8.831 +/- 0.734 ... M. villosus shows the highest rates of production of Asn (1.211 +/- 0.179), Ala (1.203 +/- 0.141), Val (1.945 +/- 0.157), and Ile (1.382 +/- 0.123
growth_records.notes: Standard conditions (Table 1): 80C, 50 mL, 2x/day gassing. H2/CO2 uptake from stoichiometry 4H2+CO2->CH4+2H2O: qH2=4*3.875=15.50; qCO2=3.875 mmol/L/h. WER/MER=2.115. No mu reported (t_inc=36.9 h, OD_end=0.700+/-0.012). Base medium DSMZ 282 (modified), full composition not stated; medium_composition_stated=false.

sec.method: reported highest MER +/- 0.79 at 80C, 25 mL, 2x/day gassing

growth_records.cite: Taubner et al. Lipidomics and Comparative Metabolite Excretion Analysis of Methanogenic Archaea Reveal Organism-Specific Adaptations to Varying Temperatures and Substrate Concentrations. mSystems (2023). DOI:10.1128/msystems.01159-22. [PMC10134847]
growth_records.snippet: The highest MERs ... at 25 mL with twice-per-day gassing ... 10.23 +/- 0.79 mmol L-1 h-1 for M. villosus.
growth_records.notes: Non-standard high-MER condition: 80C, 25 mL liquid, 2x/day gassing. MER=10.23 mmol/L/h reported. H2/CO2 uptake from stoichiometry 4H2+CO2->CH4+2H2O: qH2=4*10.23=40.92; qCO2=10.23 mmol/L/h. No mu; base medium DSMZ 282 (modified), full composition not stated.

sec.method: reported +/- 1.32 (highest Asp AAER of all experiments; 65C, 25 mL, 2x/day)
reported +/- 1.92 (highest Glu AAER of all experiments; 65C, 25 mL, 2x/day)

growth_records.cite: Taubner et al. Lipidomics and Comparative Metabolite Excretion Analysis of Methanogenic Archaea Reveal Organism-Specific Adaptations to Varying Temperatures and Substrate Concentrations. mSystems (2023). DOI:10.1128/msystems.01159-22. [PMC10134847]
growth_records.snippet: the only setting where Asp (3.82 +/- 1.32 umol L-1 h-1, with twice-per-day gassing) and Glu (5.66 +/- 1.92 umol L-1 h-1, with twice-per-day gassing) not only dominated but also showed the highest rates in all experiments performed in this s
growth_records.notes: Distinct condition: M. villosus at 65C (below its 80C optimum), 25 mL, 2x/day gassing. Only amino acid excretion rates (Asp, Glu) explicitly reported for this condition; MER/WER for this specific setting not given numerically in text (shown only in Fig. 3). No mu. Included for the two high-value AA secretion rates.
